channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Argentina protesters condemn Milei healthcare funding cuts
item.description: Hundreds marched in Buenos Aires against President Javier Milei’s austerity policies and cuts to Argentina’s healthcare

item.title: Cubans react to US indictment of former President Raul Castro
item.description: Cubans in Havana and Miami react to the US charges against former Cuban President Raul Castro.

item.title: Nvidia posts record profit of $58.3bn amid AI chip boom
item.description: Chip giant announces $80bn stock buyback scheme and dividend hike in boon to shareholders.

item.title: Indonesians sue government over deadly Sumatra flood response
item.description: Survivors of Indonesia’s deadly Sumatra floods are suing the government over inadequate disaster recovery

item.title: US lifts sanctions on Francesca Albanese, UN expert on Palestinian rights
item.description: Albanese is among several people sanctioned by the US for advocacy against Israel's genocidal war in Gaza.

item.title: Trump says no escalation expected with Cuba after Castro indictment
item.description: President Trump said the United States is “freeing up Cuba” and ruled out escalation after the US indicted Castro

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran says no surrender to US, diplomacy ‘wiser’ than war
item.description: Israeli attacks have killed 3,073 people across Lebanon since March, according to the Lebanese Health Ministry.

item.title: US police officers sue Trump over $1.8bn ‘anti-weaponisation’ fund
item.description: Officers Harry Dunn and Daniel Hodges say the fund will likely be used to 'reward' rioters who attacked the US Capitol.

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX unveils filing for blockbuster IPO
item.description: A successful sale could value the company at a record-setting $1.75 trillion.

item.title: US condemns Israel’s Ben-Gvir while sanctioning Gaza flotilla organisers
item.description: Mike Huckabee says Ben-Gvir 'betrayed dignity' in video taunting flotilla activists, day after US sanctioned organisers.

item.title: US lawyers say man on death row could be executed with expired lethal drugs
item.description: Execution method has long raised questions about cruel and unusual punishment and botched executions.

item.title: Bolivian president to reshuffle cabinet amid antigovernment protests
item.description: Rodrigo Paz is under pressure from weeks of demonstrations and poor economic conditions.

item.title: Why has the US indicted Cuba’s Raul Castro now?
item.description: The indictment of former Cuban President Raul Castro over a 1996 plane shootdown revives a decades-old case.

item.title: Israeli settlers burn vehicles, install caravans in occupied West Bank
item.description: Palestinian residents say Israeli settlers burned vehicles, sprayed racist graffiti, and installed new outposts.

item.title: How AIPAC channels millions through shell PACs ahead of US midterms
item.description: Al Jazeera analyses how pro-Israel lobby group has relied on shell PACs with deceptive names to defeat Israel critics.

item.title: James Murdoch to acquire New York Magazine and Vox Media Podcast Network
item.description: The deal, valued at more than $300m, gives Murdoch control of a storied magazine and a podcast division.

item.title: What’s the significance of the Russian president’s visit to China?
item.description: Xi Jinping hosts Vladimir Putin a week after Donald Trump was in Beijing.

item.title: China and Russia unite after Trump’s Beijing visit
item.description: Just days after Donald Trump’s visit to Beijing, Vladimir Putin arrived in China for a summit with Xi Jinping.

item.title: US indicts Cuba’s former leader Raul Castro: Why it matters
item.description: The indictment against Raul Castro echoes earlier US criminal cases targeting Latin American leaders.

item.title: US indicts Cuba’s Raul Castro over 1996 plane downing
item.description: US federal prosecutors announced the indictment of former Cuban President Raul Castro.

item.title: Sierra Leone receives first group of West African deportees from US
item.description: Sierra Leone receives nine West African migrants expelled by the United States under its strict immigration crackdown.

item.title: Chris Rabb win in Pennsylvania energises Democrats’ progressive flank
item.description: Rabb's primary win all-but assures midterm victory in US House race, setting him on course to bolster party's left.

item.title: Russia ‘dangerously’ intercepts British spy plane over Black Sea: Ministry
item.description: The incident came last month when unarmed Rivet Joint plane was securing NATO's eastern flank in international airspace.

item.title: Meta cuts 8,000 jobs in sweeping global layoffs
item.description: Meta cancels hiring plans for 6,000 roles amid ongoing layoffs and a pivot to artificial intelligence development.

item.title: Controlled reopening ends Iran’s lengthy stock market shutdown
item.description: Stocks in companies hit by US and Israeli strikes, such as energy and steel firms, didn't take part in the reopening.
